title: caladenia uliginosa subsp. patulens
intro: Caladenia uliginosa subsp. patulens, also known as the marsh caladenia, is a rare and endangered orchid species native to Western Australia. As an orchid, it has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat various skin conditions, such as wounds, cuts, and infections, as well as its ability to alleviate respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of the marsh caladenia are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
